# About Me

I am a student developer and AI/ML enthusiast focused on building intelligent software systems that combine strong engineering foundations with practical machine learning applications.

My work sits at the intersection of artificial intelligence, developer tools, and system design. I am particularly interested in building systems where machine learning models interact with structured reasoning, operating systems, and real-world workflows.

I have worked on projects involving la

rkflows.

I have worked on projects involving large language models, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, semantic search pipelines, and AI-powered developer environments. My projects often explore how AI can be integrated into everyday software systems rather than treated as isolated models.

One of my notable projects is **AInux**, an experimental AI-native Linux environment designed to integrate large language models directly into system workflows. The project explores ideas such

system workflows. The project explores ideas such as natural-language system interaction, AI-assisted shell commands, and persistent contextual memory within an operating system.

I also build intelligent document understanding systems, including LLM-powered query and retrieval platforms capable of processing PDFs, emails, and structured enterprise documents. These systems focus on extracting relevant information, matching clauses, and generating structured explanations.

Beyond AI systems,

ing structured explanations.

Beyond AI systems, I develop full-stack web applications using modern tools such as React, TypeScript, and TailwindCSS. I place strong emphasis on clean architecture, modular components, and responsive user interfaces that remain performant and maintainable.

I am motivated by building software that not only demonstrates technical capability but also explores how intelligent systems can augment real-world workflows and developer productivity.

# Education

My academic background focuses on artificial intelligence, machine learning, and intelligent software systems.

## B.Tech in Computer Science (AI & ML)

I am currently pursuing a **Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science with a specialization in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning** at **Vishwakarma Institute of Technology (VIT)**.

My studies focus on deep learning, intelligent systems, and modern AI architectures. Through this program I explore neural networks,

My experience primarily comes from building and experimenting with real-world software systems and applied AI projects.

I work across the full stack, designing both user-facing interfaces and backend systems that power intelligent applications. Many of my projects involve integrating machine learning models with practical software environments such as web applications, operating systems, and embedded devices.

# Personal Interests and Motivation

## What Inspired Me to Work in AI

I became interested in artificial intelligence because it combines several areas I enjoy: mathematics, programming, and systems thinking. What fascinates me most is the ability to build systems that can reason about information, learn from data, and assist humans in solving complex problems.

Rather than treating AI as a standalone model, I enjoy exploring how intelligent systems can be integrated into real software en

nt systems can be integrated into real software environments such as operating systems, developer tools, and interactive applications.

## What I Enjoy Building

I enjoy building systems where machine learning interacts with larger software architectures. This includes projects where AI models are embedded into developer tools, operating systems, or real-world applications.

Some of my favourite types of projects involve:

## Problems I Like Solving

I enjoy working on problems that involve both engineering and reasoning. In particular, I like designing systems that combine multiple components such as machine learning models, backend services, and user interfaces.

## What Motivates Me

What motivates me most is the process of turning complex ideas into working systems. I enjoy experimenting with new architectures, building prototypes, and refining them into practical tools.

# Projects

I build projects that explore the intersection of artificial intelligence, systems engineering, and intelligent interfaces. My work ranges from operating system–level AI agents to embedded assistive technologies and applied machine learning systems.

Many of my projects focus on combining machine learning with real-world systems, where models interact with hardware, operating systems, or structured knowledge pipelines rather than existing as isolated experiments.

## AINux

A

existing as isolated experiments.

## AINux

AINux is an AI-augmented development environment built on Debian. It embeds an intelligent agent directly into the operating system to interpret user intent and automate workflows.

The system uses a hybrid architecture combining a Go-based core with a Python-based machine learning agent. Communication between components is handled through high-performance Unix socket IPC, allowing the AI layer to interact efficiently with system processes.

T

o interact efficiently with system processes.

The project also explores concepts such as AI-assisted shell interactions, system automation through natural language, and Git-based system state snapshotting.

## OrthoLens

OrthoLens is a real-time augmented reality system designed to assist medical professionals by overlaying diagnostic indicators onto live camera feeds.

The system combines computer vision and machine learning models with AR rendering. It performs live inference using Py

AR rendering. It performs live inference using PyTorch models while rendering augmented visual cues through AR frameworks such as Unity or WebXR.

Interaction with the system is designed to be intuitive through gesture and voice-based controls.

## VisionAid

VisionAid is an offline assistive device designed for visually impaired users. The system combines computer vision with sensor-based obstacle detection on a Raspberry Pi.

It uses object detection models such as YOLO along with cla

bject detection models such as YOLO along with classical computer vision techniques like HOG for identifying nearby objects. Ultrasonic sensors provide additional environmental awareness, while an offline speech system powered by Vosk provides real-time audio feedback to the user.

The system is designed to operate fully offline, making it suitable for low-connectivity environments.

## VerifyMD

VerifyMD is an AI-powered assistant designed to support orthopedic doctors in analyzing X-ray

to support orthopedic doctors in analyzing X-ray images.

The system uses a ResNet-based convolutional neural network trained to classify orthopedic conditions with high accuracy. It integrates large language models through Google Gemini and LangChain to enable conversational interaction with medical data.

A FAISS-based vector retrieval system allows doctors to query medical knowledge and receive contextual explanations alongside model predictions.

## Vector Database Optimization System

ictions.

## Vector Database Optimization System for LLM Retrieval Pipelines

This project explores performance optimization techniques for vector databases used in large language model retrieval pipelines.

The system benchmarks different vector indexing strategies, including HNSW-based approximate nearest neighbour search, against exact search baselines. It evaluates the trade-offs between latency, recall, and throughput across different configurations.

The project aims to improve ret

configurations.

The project aims to improve retrieval performance for RAG-based systems by identifying optimal indexing parameters and database configurations for large-scale embedding workloads.
